snippetLines.lineText: /** Specific retry logic for remote execution/caching. */
/** A ResultClassifier suitable to be used by GrpcRemoteExecutor. */
/** Specific retry logic for execute request with gapi Status. */
/** Methods useful when using the {@link RemoteRetrier}. */
/** Fetches the ServerCapabilities of the remote execution/cache server. */
public class GrpcCacheClient extends RemoteCacheClient implements MissingDigestsFinder {
private static final GoogleLogger logger = GoogleLogger.forEnclosingClass();
public class GrpcRemoteDownloader implements AutoCloseable, Downloader {
private final MutableHandlerRegistry serviceRegistry = new MutableHandlerRegistry();
private final String fakeServerName = "fake server for " + getClass();
private final String serverName = "Server for " + this.getClass();
private final String fakeServerName = "fake server for " + getClass();
private final StoredEventHandler eventHandler = new StoredEventHandler();
public final void setUp() throws Exception {
// Use a mutable service registry for later registering the service impl for each test case.
/** Output service implementation for the remote build with local output service daemon. */
// Send a partial lists to avoid too large messages.
* Creates the instance of the {@link Retrier.CircuitBreaker} as per the strategy defined in
* {@link RemoteOptions}. In case of undefined strategy defaults to {@link
* The {@link FailureCircuitBreaker} implementation of the {@link Retrier.CircuitBreaker} prevents
* further calls to a remote cache once the failures rate within a given window exceeds a specified
* threshold for a build. In the context of Bazel, a new instance of {@link Retrier.CircuitBreaker}
* is created for each build. Therefore, if the circuit breaker trips during a build, the remote
* cache will be disabled for that build. However, it will be enabled again for the next build as a
* new instance of {@link Retrier.CircuitBreaker} will be created.
// Suppress unchecked warnings because any(Callable.class) uses a raw type,
// which causes Javac to fail with -Werror.
private final MutableHandlerRegistry serviceRegistry = new MutableHandlerRegistry();
private final String fakeServerName = "fake server for " + getClass();
/** A factory class for providing a {@link CombinedCacheClient}. */
@Rule public final RxNoGlobalErrorsRule rxNoGlobalErrorsRule = new RxNoGlobalErrorsRule();

*

This class is not thread safe, but it is safe to message pass its objects between threads.
